steps.title: Use the visual object
steps.content: The speaker says, Once again / Do I behold these steep and lofty cliffs. The verb is directed at a visible landscape, so it describes the speaker's renewed act of seeing, viewing, or attending to the cliffs.

steps.title: Eliminate nonvisual meanings
steps.content: Choice B, Possess, means to own or have something, but the speaker is not claiming ownership of the cliffs. Choice C, Escape, means to get away, which contradicts returning to the scene. Choice D, Neglect, means to ignore, which contradicts focused perception.

steps.title: Match the perception meaning
steps.content: The remaining meaning must fit the line as an action performed toward the cliffs: the speaker beholds them again. Regard can mean to look at with attention or consideration, so it matches the poem's sense of deliberate visual attention. The answer is A.
